q01.name: The Breaker in the Dark
q01.desc: Something big moved into the worked seams and it does not share. The mine masters want their tunnels back.
q01.epilogue: The seams ring with picks again by week's end.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Ghor, a orc disgraced soldier, level 15, out there somewhere)

q02.name: The Grudge War
q02.desc: A rival clan pressed an old grudge with new axes. The ledger wants balancing.
q02.epilogue: The ledger line is struck through in both clans' books -- balanced, the keeper says, for a generation at least.

q03.name: The Dragon's Tithe
q03.desc: A whole valley pays it tribute and calls that peace. The crown calls it rebellion with wings.
q03.epilogue: The valley keeps its harvest for the first year in living memory; the tithe-barns are rebuilt as granaries.

q04.name: The Giant of the Border Marches
q04.desc: Border forts flattened, garrisons walking home weaponless. The marshal wants the marches quiet again.
q04.epilogue: The forts are re-garrisoned; the marches go quiet, and stay so.

q05.name: The Rune-Fire Below
q05.desc: Chanters feeding forbidden runes in a sealed gallery. The runesmiths want the fire out before the stone learns it.
q05.epilogue: The vault is re-sealed under three locks and a new rune; the warden files the party's names under 'safe hands'.

q06.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q06.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q06.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q07.name: The Coven in the Mists
q07.desc: Singers who left the circle and took the old songs with them. The mists over the hollow are not weather.
q07.epilogue: The mists lift over the hollow; the censor seals the stolen songs back into the circle's keeping, and does not say thank you.

q08.name: Webs in the Canopy
q08.desc: The high walkways are silked over and the wardens who went up have not come down.
q08.epilogue: The silk is cut away and the walkways rehung; the lost wardens are carried down for their rites.

giver.name: Jelenneth
giver.role: the master of the high walkways

q09.name: The Coven in the Mists
q09.desc: Singers who left the circle and took the old songs with them. The mists over the hollow are not weather.
q09.epilogue: The mists lift over the hollow; the censor seals the stolen songs back into the circle's keeping, and does not say thank you.

q10.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q10.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.
q10.epilogue: The recovered blades go back to the armory; the villages post no watch tonight.

q11.name: The Restless Crypt
q11.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q11.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q12.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q12.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.
q12.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

q13.name: The Hedge-Wizards' Toll
q13.desc: Renegade hedge-wizards tax the road in fire and frost. The witchfinder pays for the tollhouse dark.
q13.epilogue: Carters use the road again by week's end; the witchfinder burns the confiscated grimoires in the market square, twice, for the crowd.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Osric, a human rival adventurer, level 10, out there somewhere)

q14.name: Spiders in the Undercity
q14.desc: The vent shafts are webbed shut and the air below is going bad. Small pay, small heroes welcome.
q14.epilogue: Air moves in the undercity again; small pay, but the vent-warden's word is now good in every burrow.

q15.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q15.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q15.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q16.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q16.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.
q16.epilogue: The lower works are re-lit gallery by gallery; the bosses are already arguing about building it again, bigger.

q17.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q17.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.
q17.epilogue: The lower works are re-lit gallery by gallery; the bosses are already arguing about building it again, bigger.

q18.name: Scrap-Hounds Loose in the Works
q18.desc: The bosses' hound-pack slipped its chains and dens in the gearworks. Pay docked for every eaten shift-worker.
q18.epilogue: The works run a full shift without a scream; the foreman docks nobody, this once.

q19.name: The Boiler-Cult
q19.desc: A cult that worships the works' oldest boiler and feeds it what it asks for. It has started asking for shift-workers.
q19.epilogue: The old boiler is decommissioned with full honors and sold for scrap; the inspector stamps the paperwork DONE with visible pleasure.

q20.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q20.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q20.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Urzag, a orc duelist, level 10, out there somewhere)

q21.name: The Coven in the Mists
q21.desc: Singers who left the circle and took the old songs with them. The mists over the hollow are not weather.
q21.epilogue: The mists lift over the hollow; the censor seals the stolen songs back into the circle's keeping, and does not say thank you.

q22.name: Wolves at the Folds
q22.desc: Sheep gone, then a shepherd. The commons want the pack dug out of the hills.
q22.epilogue: Lambing season passes without a beast lost; the elder salts the den's earth as the old rites ask.

q23.name: The Restless Crypt
q23.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q23.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q24.name: The Boiler-Cult
q24.desc: A cult that worships the works' oldest boiler and feeds it what it asks for. It has started asking for shift-workers.
q24.epilogue: The old boiler is decommissioned with full honors and sold for scrap; the inspector stamps the paperwork DONE with visible pleasure.

q25.name: The Coven in the Mists
q25.desc: Singers who left the circle and took the old songs with them. The mists over the hollow are not weather.
q25.epilogue: The mists lift over the hollow; the censor seals the stolen songs back into the circle's keeping, and does not say thank you.

q26.name: Beasts Maddened by the Blight
q26.desc: Boar and bear alike gone wrong-eyed, tearing through the outlying groves.
q26.epilogue: The groves go quiet again; the keeper plants a sapling for every beast that had to die.

q27.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q27.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q27.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q28.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q28.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q28.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Keyleth, age 10)

q29.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q29.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.
q29.epilogue: The recovered blades go back to the armory; the villages post no watch tonight.

q30.name: The Flooded Hold
q30.desc: An old hold, lost and lately dry again. What was buried with it did not stay buried.
q30.epilogue: The heir walks the dry galleries once, dry-eyed, and seals the hold behind -- some doors are better shut.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Baern, a dwarf disgraced soldier, level 3, out there somewhere)

q31.name: The Renegade Magus
q31.desc: A court wizard who stopped asking leave. The crown pays for the tower dark and the fires out.
q31.epilogue: The tower stands dark and cold; the crown's sage carries the renegade's books away under seal.

q32.name: The Blighted Glade
q32.desc: Something soured the heartwood; what lives there now bites. The wardens want it cut out.
q32.epilogue: Green returns to the glade's edge within the season; what soured the heartwood is burned and sung over.

q33.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q33.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q33.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q34.name: Spiders in the Undercity
q34.desc: The vent shafts are webbed shut and the air below is going bad. Small pay, small heroes welcome.
q34.epilogue: Air moves in the undercity again; small pay, but the vent-warden's word is now good in every burrow.

q35.desc: An old hold, lost and lately dry again. What was buried with it did not stay buried.
q35.epilogue: The heir walks the dry galleries once, dry-eyed, and seals the hold behind -- some doors are better shut.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Gardain, age 10)

q36.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q36.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q36.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q37.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q37.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q37.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q38.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q38.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q38.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q39.name: Scrap-Hounds Loose in the Works
q39.desc: The bosses' hound-pack slipped its chains and dens in the gearworks. Pay docked for every eaten shift-worker.
q39.epilogue: The works run a full shift without a scream; the foreman docks nobody, this once.

q40.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q40.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.
q40.epilogue: The recovered blades go back to the armory; the villages post no watch tonight.

q41.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q41.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.
q41.epilogue: The lower works are re-lit gallery by gallery; the bosses are already arguing about building it again, bigger.

q42.name: Ashes Going Home
q42.desc: One of theirs died far from home. The family wants the ashes carried the whole way, and carried right.
q42.epilogue: The urn goes into the ground beside its people; the kin set a place at the table for the party that night.

q43.name: The Sealed Dispatch
q43.desc: Court business under wax, and a rider who never arrived. Someone would rather it never does.
q43.epilogue: The seal is broken behind a shut door; whatever it says, couriers ride the same road twice as guarded within the week.

q44.name: Sack the Village
q44.desc: A whole village, one night, everything in it: all their gold is yours. They have a palisade, a militia, and strong opinions about visitors with torches.
q44.epilogue: The village will rebuild, the ballads insist. The ballads are mostly about what the party took, listed, with amounts.

q45.name: The Last Sermon
q45.desc: Hell wants the local priest on the altar he preaches from. He is, for the record, a bad man -- a usurer in vestments who has ruined more families than any bandit. Will you still chicken out?
q45.epilogue: The parish, freed of its usurer, is oddly quiet about the how. The ledger of debts burns in the vestry grate, and nobody claims to have lit it.

q46.name: The Blade That Thirsts
q46.desc: A famous evil weapon lies in the barrow of the last fool who wielded it. Hell would like it back in circulation. The barrow disagrees.
q46.epilogue: The blade comes up humming, faintly, in a key that makes dogs leave the room. It is very pleased to meet you. (The DM places the actual weapon -- a reskinned quality blade until named instances land.)

q47.name: The Vault Job
q47.desc: The counting-house vault holds three lands' worth of deposits. The plan is elegant. The guards are not part of the plan.

deed.text: the plan -- cased for a week, timed to the minute, in through the lamplit gallery between rounds
deed.fail: the rounds changed this morning; the plan meets the guards

q47.epilogue: Three counting-houses fail by month's end and the broker, who sold their debts short, sends a fruit basket. It is not poisoned. Probably.

w1.name: Strange Salvage
w1.desc: Livestock, fence-iron, and one census-taker have gone missing along the elf border. What the trackers found was half animal, half machine, and all appetite.
w1.epilogue: The burrow is collapsed onto its own bomb stores; the crater smokes for a week and the livestock stop vanishing.

q49.name: The Vault Job
q49.desc: The counting-house vault holds three lands' worth of deposits. The plan is elegant. The guards are not part of the plan.

deed.text: the plan -- cased for a week, timed to the minute, in through the lamplit gallery between rounds
deed.fail: the rounds changed this morning; the plan meets the guards

q49.epilogue: Three counting-houses fail by month's end and the broker, who sold their debts short, sends a fruit basket. It is not poisoned. Probably.

has a child (with them: Dizzy, age 8)
